title: pyrola rotundifolia subsp. maritima
intro: Pyrola rotundifolia subsp. maritima, also known as sea wintergreen, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the heath family (Ericaceae) that is native to coastal areas in the Northern Hemisphere. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving respiratory issues, as well as having antiviral and antibacterial properties. In herbal preparations, the leaves and stems of Pyrola rotundifolia subsp. maritima are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and potential interactions.
